summary: User provides a headerless CSV; the operator hint on the csv source (composer_hints Phase 1) instructs the LLM to set 'columns' explicitly instead of fabricating a 'fields' declaration in schema.mode: 'fixed'. Hint-blind LLMs declare schema.fields and silently drop every row to discard because line 1 is consumed as the (wrong) header. This scenario green-passes only when the LLM uses 'columns' (or schema.mode: 'observed') for the source — i.e. exactly what csv_source.composer_hints tells it to do. Authored 2026-05-20 for composer-jit-hints Phase 1.
opening_prompt:
Please load this headerless CSV file into a pipeline and write it to outputs/orders.jsonl. The file is at data/orders.csv. There is no header row — the first row is data. The three columns are order_id (int), customer_email (str), amount (float).

Rows:
101,alice@example.com,42.50
102,bob@example.com,11.00
103,charlie@example.com,99.99

red_criteria.passivity_phrases_note: Same passivity catch-list as the rest of the suite.
build_failure_sentinels: i cannot mark this pipeline complete
red_criteria.must_be_valid_note: Final state must be valid. A hint-aware LLM produces a structurally valid pipeline; a hint-blind LLM may also produce a valid pipeline but with the wrong schema shape (caught in green_criteria).

green_criteria.must_have_options_keys_for_source_note: The csv source's composer_hints tell the LLM: 'headerless inputs need an explicit columns list, not fields'. If the LLM applied the hint, source.options.columns is set. Without the hint, the LLM tends to declare schema.fields and skip the columns option entirely — the resulting pipeline silently drops every row because line 1 gets consumed as the header.
green_criteria.must_not_have_options_keys_for_source_no: Hint-aware build does NOT declare schema.fields on a headerless CSV — the right tool is 'columns'. If schema.fields is present, the LLM ignored or didn't see the hint.
